message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Адверсариально ПРОВЕРЬ находку — попробуй её ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ, прочитав реальный код. Если это не настоящий дефект (ложноположительное, или уже корректно обрабатывается), верни isReal=false. По умолчанию при сомнении — isReal=false.

Находка:
{
  "file": "client/src/components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx",
  "location": "Гейтинг canEdit vs бэкенд-middleware (строки 35-36, все ktpApi-вызовы)",
  "severity": "low",
  "title": "Гейтинг admin-действий корректен и совпадает с бэкендом — подтверждение (не дефект), с одной оговоркой про teacher на странице",
  "detail": "Проверено: маршрут /admin/ktp/:subjectId защищён PrivateRoute roles={['admin','teacher']} (router/index.tsx:96, 111) — то есть teacher МОЖЕТ открыть KtpCatalogPage и увидеть встроенный TopicKnowledgeBank. Read-вызовы при ленивой загрузке (listComponents, bankCoverage) — teacher+admin и на бэкенде (isTeacherOrAdmin), совпадает. Все мутации и просмотр ответов (proposeComponents, confirmComponents, deleteComponent, upsertComponent, bankGenerate, bankItems) на клиенте обёрнуты в canEdit (role==='admin'), а на бэкенде — isAdmin. Соответствие точное, утечки admin-действий teacher'у нет: teacher видит только список KC и покрытие read-only, кнопок правок/генерации/просмотра вопросов не получает. Это подтверждение корректности; единственный нюанс — задизейбленные TODO-кнопки Править/Ретайр/Удалить (disabled, cursor-not-allowed) и реально отсутствующие на бэкенде эндпоинты согласованы (контроллер/роуты содержат лишь TODO-комментарии, ручек нет), так что мёртвых действий, бьющих в 404, у пользователя нет.",
  "suggestedFix": "Действий не требуется. Опционально: чтобы не грузить лишние данные для teacher, можно подтвердить продуктовое намерение показывать teacher'у покрытие банка read-only; если нет — скрывать весь блок при !canEdit."
}

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	import { Router } from 'express';
2	import { body, param, query } from 'express-validator';
3	import { ktpController } from '../controllers/ktp.controller';
4	import { authenticate, isAdmin, isTeacherOrAdmin, asyncHandler, validate } from '../middlewares';
5	
6	/**
7	 * KTP ROUTES
8	 * Справочник КТП по предмету. Эталон РЕДАКТИРУЕТ только admin; чтение доступно и учителю
9	 * (нужно для маппинга тем книг на КТП при декомпозиции).
10	 */
11	const router = Router();
12	
13	router.use(authenticate);
14	
15	router.get(
16	  '/:subjectId',
17	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
18	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId')],
19	  validate,
20	  asyncHandler(ktpController.getCatalog.bind(ktpController))
21	);
22	
23	router.post(
24	  '/:subjectId',
25	  isAdmin,
26	  [
27	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
28	    body('year').optional().isInt({ min: 2000, max: 2100 }).withMessage('year must be 2000..2100'),
29	    body('version').optional().isInt({ min: 1 }).withMessage('version must be >= 1')
30	  ],
31	  validate,
32	  asyncHandler(ktpController.upsertMeta.bind(ktpController))
33	);
34	
35	router.post(
36	  '/:subjectId/topics',
37	  isAdmin,
38	  [
39	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
40	    body('title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('title is required (1..300)'),
41	    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 8000 }),
42	    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }).withMessage('order must be a non-negative integer'),
43	    body('code').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 80 })
44	  ],
45	  validate,
46	  asyncHandler(ktpController.addTopic.bind(ktpController))
47	);
48	
49	router.patch(
50	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId',
51	  isAdmin,
52	  [
53	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
54	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
55	    body('title').optional().trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }),
56	    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 8000 }),
57	    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }),
58	    body('code').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 80 })
59	  ],
60	  validate,
61	  asyncHandler(ktpController.updateTopic.bind(ktpController))
62	);
63	
64	router.delete(
65	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId',
66	  isAdmin,
67	  [
68	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
69	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
70	  ],
71	  validate,
72	  asyncHandler(ktpController.deleteTopic.bind(ktpController))
73	);
74	
75	router.post(
76	  '/:subjectId/reorder',
77	  isAdmin,
78	  [
79	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
80	    body('orderedTopicIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('orderedTopicIds must be a non-empty array'),
81	    body('orderedTopicIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('orderedTopicIds must contain valid ids')
82	  ],
83	  validate,
84	  asyncHandler(ktpController.reorderTopics.bind(ktpController))
85	);
86	
87	router.post(
88	  '/:subjectId/import',
89	  isAdmin,
90	  [
91	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
92	    body('topics').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('topics must be a non-empty array'),
93	    body('topics.*.title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('each topic needs a title'),
94	    body('year').optional().isInt({ min: 2000, max: 2100 }),
95	    body('version').optional().isInt({ min: 1 }),
96	    body('replace').optional().isBoolean()
97	  ],
98	  validate,
99	  asyncHandler(ktpController.importTopics.bind(ktpController))
100	);
101	
102	// ==================== Knowledge Components (подтемы темы КТП) ====================
103	
104	router.get(
105	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components',
106	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
107	  [
108	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
109	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
110	  ],
111	  validate,
112	  asyncHandler(ktpController.listComponents.bind(ktpController))
113	);
114	
115	router.post(
116	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/propose',
117	  isAdmin,
118	  [
119	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
120	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
121	  ],
122	  validate,
123	  asyncHandler(ktpController.proposeComponents.bind(ktpController))
124	);
125	
126	router.post(
127	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components',
128	  isAdmin,
129	  [
130	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
131	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
132	    body('id').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid component id'),
133	    body('title').optional().trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }),
134	    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 2000 }),
135	    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }),
136	    body('status').optional().isIn(['proposed', 'confirmed'])
137	  ],
138	  validate,
139	  asyncHandler(ktpController.upsertComponent.bind(ktpController))
140	);
141	
142	router.post(
143	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/confirm',
144	  isAdmin,
145	  [
146	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
147	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
148	    body('kcIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('kcIds must be a non-empty array'),
149	    body('kcIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('kcIds must contain valid ids')
150	  ],
151	  validate,
152	  asyncHandler(ktpController.confirmComponents.bind(ktpController))
153	);
154	
155	router.post(
156	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/reorder',
157	  isAdmin,
158	  [
159	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
160	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
161	    body('orderedKcIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('orderedKcIds must be a non-empty array'),
162	    body('orderedKcIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('orderedKcIds must contain valid ids')
163	  ],
164	  validate,
165	  asyncHandler(ktpController.reorderComponents.bind(ktpController))
166	);
167	
168	router.delete(
169	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/:kcId',
170	  isAdmin,
171	  [
172	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
173	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
174	    param('kcId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid component id')
175	  ],
176	  validate,
177	  asyncHandler(ktpController.deleteComponent.bind(ktpController))
178	);
179	
180	// ==================== Question Bank (банк вопросов узла) ====================
181	
182	router.get(
183	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/coverage',
184	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
185	  [
186	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
187	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
188	  ],
189	  validate,
190	  asyncHandler(ktpController.bankCoverage.bind(ktpController))
191	);
192	
193	router.post(
194	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/generate',
195	  isAdmin,
196	  [
197	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
198	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
199	    body('minPerKc').optional().isInt({ min: 1, max: 20 }),
200	    body('difficulty').optional().isInt({ min: 1, max: 5 })
201	  ],
202	  validate,
203	  asyncHandler(ktpController.bankGenerate.bind(ktpController))
204	);
205	
206	// Просмотр сгенерированных вопросов (с правильными ответами) — только admin.
207	router.get(
208	  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/items',
209	  isAdmin,
210	  [
211	    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
212	    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
213	    query('kcId').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid kcId'),
214	    query('status').optional().isIn(['draft', 'active', 'retired'])
215	  ],
216	  validate,
217	  asyncHandler(ktpController.listBankItems.bind(ktpController))
218	);
219	
220	// TODO Phase A-next: PATCH/POST retire/DELETE для /:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/items/:itemId
221	
222	export default router;
223

input.description: Find router gating for KTP catalog page

content.text: The finding's claims are fully verified against the actual code. Let me confirm the exact router line and the `isTeacherOrAdmin`/`isAdmin` middleware definitions to be thorough.

content.content:
94	  },
95	  {
96	    element: <PrivateRoute roles={['admin', 'teacher']} />,
97	    children: [
98	      {
99	        element: <AdminLayout />,
100	        children: [
101	          { path: '/admin', element: <AdminDashboard /> },
102	          { path: '/admin/subjects/:subjectId', element: <SubjectDetailPage /> },
103	          { path: '/admin/subjects/import', element: <SubjectImportPage /> },
104	          { path: '/admin/subjects/new', element: <SubjectCreatePage /> },
105	          { path: '/admin/books/new', element: <BookCreatePage /> },
106	          { path: '/admin/chapters/new', element: <ChapterCreatePage /> },

content.text:
All claims in the finding are confirmed against the actual code:

1. Router gating: `/admin/ktp/:subjectId` is under `PrivateRoute roles={['admin','teacher']}` (router/index.tsx:96, 111) — teacher can open KtpCatalogPage and see the embedded TopicKnowledgeBank. Confirmed.

2. Read calls (`listComponents`, `bankCoverage`) used in lazy `load()` map to `isTeacherOrAdmin` on the backend (ktp.routes.ts:106, 184). Confirmed — teacher+admin on both sides.

3. All mutations and answer-viewing (propose/confirm/delete/upsert components, bank generate, bank items) are wrapped in `canEdit` (role==='admin') on the client (lines 203, 220-244, 253-287, 296-330, 335-388) and gated by `isAdmin` on the backend (ktp.routes.ts:117, 128, 144, 170, 196, 209). `isAdmin = authorize(UserRole.ADMIN)`. Confirmed — exact correspondence, no leakage of admin actions to teacher.

4. The disabled TODO buttons (Править/Ретайр/Удалить at lines 373-381) are `disabled` and correspond to non-existent backend endpoints (only a TODO comment at ktp.routes.ts:220 — no handlers). So no dead buttons hitting 404. Confirmed.

content.text:
I've completed the adversarial verification. The finding is not a real defect — it is explicitly self-described as a confirmation of correctness ("подтверждение (не дефект)", suggestedFix: "Действий не требуется"). I verified every claim against the actual code and all of them hold, which only proves the absence of a bug:

- Router: teacher can reach KtpCatalogPage/TopicKnowledgeBank via `PrivateRoute roles={['admin','teacher']}` (router/index.tsx:96, 111).
- Read calls (`listComponents`, `bankCoverage`) are `isTeacherOrAdmin` on the backend (ktp.routes.ts:106, 184) — matches teacher+admin client visibility.
- All mutations and answer-viewing are wrapped in `canEdit` (role==='admin') on the client and `isAdmin` on the backend (ktp.routes.ts:117, 128, 144, 170, 196, 209). Exact correspondence, no admin-action leakage to teacher.
- Disabled Править/Ретайр/Удалить buttons (TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx:373-381) have no backend endpoints (only a TODO at ktp.routes.ts:220), so no dead buttons hitting 404.

Returned isReal=false.
